title: Track Daily Spending
summary: See where your money is going by tracking what you spend.
learnerPromise: See where your money is going by tracking what you spend.
whyItMatters: Most people do not know where their money goes. Tracking spending is the first step to taking control of your finances. It is eye-opening and empowering.
realLifeUses: Find out if small daily purchases are adding up to a big monthly total.
Identify one category where you can cut back.
Prove to yourself that you can stick to a money habit.
Build the foundation for creating your first budget.
youWillLearn: Choose a tracking method — notebook, spreadsheet, or app.
Record purchases with amount and what they were for.
Group expenses into categories like food, transport, and entertainment.
Review your data and spot your biggest spending area.
miniChallenge: Write down your last 5 purchases and group them by category.
steps: Pick your tracking tool — a notes app, a notebook, or a budgeting app.
Set three simple categories: needs, wants, and savings.
Write down your last 5 purchases with amount and what each was for.
Label each purchase into one of your three categories.
Look at the results. What category had the most spending?
completionCriteria: You have 5 purchases recorded with amounts and categories.
You can name your top spending category.
commonProblems: You forget to record a purchase — make it a habit to record immediately, not later.
Cash is harder to track than cards — keep receipts or jot it down right away.
You feel guilty about some purchases — remember, tracking is not judging, it is just data.
tips: Round to the nearest dollar — exact cents do not matter at this stage.
Be honest. The only person seeing this is you.
Set a daily phone reminder to review your spending each evening.
